results.name: piotr rasputin
results.description: From Marvel Comics, an X-man better known as Colossus.

results.name: pip-boy
results.description: Arm mounted computing device, from the Fallout universe

results.name: pitcher plant
results.description: A carnivorous plant with an opening at the top

results.name: pitch spell
results.description: Cards that let you discard or exile another card from your hand to cast for less or no mana

results.name: pizza
results.description: For actual pizzas... and things that sorta look like pizzas.

results.name: plagiarized
results.description: Art that has been found to potentially be plagiarised by copying or kitbashing other artworks without permission.

results.name: planar origin
results.description: Tags classifying the origins of creatures and objects from across the multiverse.

results.name: plane
results.description: A collection of art tags that relate to specific planes.

results.name: planeswalker
results.description: A character who is, or was, a planeswalker.

results.name: planeswalker deck staples
results.description: Cards that are formulaic includes of planeswalker decks: [tutors](/tags/card/pwdeck-tutor) to help you find the deck's planeswalker, and [sidekicks](/tags/card/pwdeck-sidekick) that benefit from having that planeswalker out.

results.name: planeswalker symbol
results.description: The planeswalker symbol in art. Don't use this for the planeswalker stamp.

results.name: plant creature
results.description: Like a regular creature, but plant. See also [plantfolk](plantfolk) for humanoid plants.

results.name: plantfolk
results.description: Anthropomorphic plants—treefolk and the like. See also [fun guy](fun-guy) for anthropomorphic fungi.

results.name: plate armor
results.description: Plates strapped to the body. also known by the misnomer of platemail.

results.name: platinum angel effect
results.description: Cards that stop you specifically from losing while preventing your opponents from winning.

results.name: players outside game matter
results.description: Cards that involve people beyond the players in the game.

results.name: player spotlight
results.description: Player Spotlight cards are Magic: The Gathering cards that celebrate the winners of the World Championships, similar to [Invitational Cards](invitational-card) that were made in the years 1997–2007.

results.name: player spotlight art
results.description: Cards that depict the current world champion at the time they were printed

results.name: play from top
results.description: Cards that let you play or cast the top card of your library. (Separate from [impulsive draw](https://tagger.scryfall.com/tags/card/impulsive-draw) which exiles it first.)

results.name: playtest forecast
results.description: These playtest cards previewed mechanics seen within 2-3 years of release, meaning they were being worked on at the time!

results.name: plunder
results.description: Sacrifice something else to draw cards.

results.name: podium
results.description: A surface on which to place papers and scrolls, often while making a speech.

results.name: poggers
results.description: Mouth agape expression reminiscent of the original version of the twitch.tv emote "Pogchamp"

results.name: poison mechanics
results.description: Cards and effects that interact with poison counters by name.

results.name: poison opponents
results.description: Gives poison counters directly to opponents, preferebly to all at once, without damage.

results.name: poisonous
results.description: Makes, or is, a creature that poisons a player when it hits them.

results.name: polearm
results.description: A family of melee weapons where the business-end is affixed at the end of a long pole.
